PHASE:        Phase 7 Feature Update — §210 Super Admin Panel backlog

              AreaBoundary derivation algorithm — closes 5.1d Area A loop.

              Tonight's two-commit ship (2026-06-01 07:48-07:52 GMT+8):

                chore (commit 3b6f729):
                  packages/db lacked @types/node in its own devDeps. Previously
                  satisfied by pnpm root hoist from apps/web / packages/storage;
                  a recent pnpm install stopped hoisting it, breaking
                  `pnpm typecheck` at root (regression undetected until Phase 7
                  hard gate ran for this work). Added `@types/node ^22.10.5`
                  matching apps/web's pin. Root typecheck now exits 0.
                  Pre-D feature work — Option D hardening (commit 19e4d4a)
                  refuses to ship feature work over a failing gate.

                feat (commit 3794c10) — AreaBoundary derivation algorithm:
                  Wired enqueueAreaRederive in er-sync.processor.ts after every
                  Event AND Patrol upsert. ER payload contains no areaName field
                  (per memory ingest 2984) — derivation falls back to
                  nearest-boundary via lat/lon for Events (which have coords);
                  Patrols use name-only match (no coords) and return null if
                  unmatched. Closes the deferred 5.1d Area A item parked in
                  queues/types.ts L51-54.
                  Sentinel: reused existing `userId: "system"` convention from
                  enqueueAlert (er-sync.processor.ts:196). queues/types.ts L40-41
                  schema docs anticipated this wiring as "sync-system sentinel
                  for automatic enqueues". applyAreaDerivation explicitly skips
                  AuditLog writes for automatic contexts (area-derivation.ts
                  L16-19), so sentinel never hits the User FK.
                  Restructured syncEvents to thread `eventId` through both
                  create + update branches — area-rederive enqueues once per
                  iteration regardless of path. Alert enqueue semantics preserved
                  (still only on create per tests L169 + L185).
                  Verify: typecheck + lint + test 710 web + 122 jobs + Next.js
                  build all exit 0. V32 R1 honored — 4 Sonnet dispatches (D1
                  production edit, D2 test extension, D3 chore @types/node,
                  D4 feature commit+push). Opus wrote only STATE.md.
                  Net: +31L production / +20L tests / +1L packages/db/package.json.

              Tonight's two-dispatch ship (2026-05-31 22:30-22:51 GMT+8):

                C-followup (commit e203597):
                  Tightened `prisma as unknown as any` → `prisma as unknown as PrismaClient`
                  at the two writeAuditLog call sites in patrolSchedule.ts (lines 138, 222)
                  added by 5dfa8f5. Sonnet also re-exported PrismaClient via
                  @marine-guardian/db (cleaner than adding @prisma/client direct dep to
                  apps/web) and removed 2 now-stale eslint-disable-next-line directives.
                  Verify: typecheck + lint + targeted vitest = 707/707. Net ~5L.

                B — Item 7 follow-up (commit 95b4659):
                  Extended platformUser.updateRole to accept optional tenantId for
                  cross-tenant user reassignment from /admin/users EditUserRoleDialog.
                  Server semantics:
                    - tenantId undefined → preserve existing (backward compat regression test)
                    - tenantId null      → reassign to platform scope (super_admin only)
                    - tenantId string    → reassign to that tenant (verified exists + isActive)
                  Audit log changesJson captures before/after tenantId only when
                  reassignment actually happens. Role-vs-tenant validation now runs
                  against the effective post-merge tenantId.
                  EditUserRoleDialog: added Tenant Select mirroring create-user-dialog
                  pattern. role=super_admin auto-sets tenantId=null + disables the select.
                  "Platform (no tenant)" option uses `__platform__` sentinel.
                  +3 router tests (reassign happy path with audit assertion, missing
                  tenant 404, undefined-preserves regression). Existing happy-path test
                  signature updated to include tenantId in return shape.
                  Verify: typecheck + lint + test 710/710 + Next.js build all clean.
                  V32 R1 honored — 2 Sonnet dispatches (B1 router+tests, B2 dialog+verify
                  +commit). Opus wrote only STATE.md.

                  PASSED end-to-end @ 23:55 GMT+8:

                  In-session defect resolved (NOT an Item 2 bug):
                    First /admin/tenants load surfaced 4× HTTP 403 on
                    platform.list. Root cause: stale `mg-impersonate-tenant`
                    HttpOnly cookie persisted from Item 4 prior QA on
                    2026-05-30. protectedProcedure detected
                    `isPlatformImpersonating = true` (super_admin +
                    sessionTenantId="" + cookie present) →
                    effectiveTenantId = impersonatedTenantId.
                    platformAdminProcedure gate (require-platform-admin.ts
                    L16) requires `ctx.tenantId === ""` → 403.

                    Sign-out + sign-in DID NOT clear it — auth/JWT cookie
                    and impersonation cookie are separate per Item 4
                    design. Fix: called platformImpersonation.exit
                    mutation via fetch → `{wasImpersonating: true}` →
                    cookie cleared → /admin/tenants 200, table populated.

                    Procedural memory added:
                    [[feedback_stale_impersonation_cookie_blocks_admin_tenants]]
                    — Item 4 QA leaves cookie behind; future Super Admin
                    Panel QAs must call platformImpersonation.exit before
                    expecting platform-scope tRPC procedures to succeed.

                  Open product question (low priority, NOT a defect):
                    Should sign-out also exit impersonation? Current
                    behavior is a defense-in-depth smell — a super_admin
                    signing out and a different super_admin signing in
                    would inherit prior impersonation state. Bundle into
                    future Item 4 polish dispatch if revisited.

LAST_DONE:    Executed Option C per prior STATE.md NEXT recommendation.
              Two-step ship with one revert mid-flight:

              STEP 1 — feat 5dfa8f5 (Sonnet dispatch 1):
                apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/patrolSchedule.ts (+53/-1)
                  - Added `writeAuditLog` to the @marine-guardian/db
                    import (existing `prisma` import on L6).
                  - `create` mutation: when `input.overrideConflicts ===
                    true`, after the existing conflict-check block,
                    do the create then write an AuditLog row with
                    action="PATROL_SCHEDULE:OVERRIDE_CONFLICT",
                    entityType="PatrolSchedule", entityId=created.id,
                    changesJson={rangerUserId, scheduledStart,
                    scheduledEnd}. Non-override path unchanged.
                  - `update` mutation: same pattern — when override
                    flag set, do the update then write audit row with
                    the resolved data field as changesJson (Dates
                    converted to ISO strings).
                apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/__tests__/patrolSchedule.test.ts (+46/-1)
                  - Mocked writeAuditLog at module level.
                  - 2 existing override tests now assert
                    `writeAuditLog` was called with the correct
                    action string.
                  - 2 new tests assert NO audit write on non-override
                    success paths (create + update).
                docs/CHANGELOG_AI.md (+9/-0) — entry prepended.

                HARD GATE results: typecheck 0 errors → 707/707 vitest
                passing → `pnpm --filter @marine-guardian/web build`
                clean. All three Phase 7 Step 19 commands exit 0.

                Tradeoff: writeAuditLog called sequentially after
                mutation (non-atomic). Argument cast as
                `prisma as unknown as any` because the tenant-guarded
                extended client doesn't trivially satisfy the helper's
                `PrismaClient | TransactionClient` signature.

              STEP 2 — refactor 7853f18 (Sonnet dispatch 2):
                Intended to wrap both override branches in
                `prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => ...)` to make the
                create+audit atomic and remove the `as any` cast.
                Sonnet instead switched to `platformPrisma.$transaction`
                — citing platform.ts L281 as precedent.

              STEP 3 — REVERT 7a11af8 (Opus, via git revert):
                platformPrisma is the BARE PrismaClient with NO
                extensions (packages/db/src/client.ts L23):
                  `export const platformPrisma = new PrismaClient();`
                while `prisma` is `.$extends(encryption).$extends(
                tenantGuardExtension)`.

                Using platformPrisma inside coordinatorProcedure
                (tenant-scoped, ctx.tenantId session-derived) BYPASSES
                the L6 Prisma guardrail extension on the override-
                mutation path only. This violates:
                  - Rule 7 L6 — "always active" tenant guardrails
                  - security.md "SUPERADMIN AND PLATFORM-LEVEL ROLES":
                    "Superadmin queries that bypass tenant scoping
                    MUST use a dedicated Prisma client instance
                    WITHOUT the L6 tenant-guard extension ...
                    Regular tenant-scoped routers continue using the
                    guarded prisma instance."

                platform.ts L281 is the legitimate use of
                platformPrisma: it CREATES a brand-new tenant + admin
                from a superadmin context (tenantId=null on the
                audit row). patrolSchedule mutations are tenant-scoped
                operations on existing tenants — they MUST use
                tenant-guarded prisma.

                Reverted via `git revert --no-edit 7853f18`. Working
                tree at 7a11af8 is byte-identical to 5dfa8f5
                (`git diff 5dfa8f5..HEAD --stat` = empty).

              FINAL STATE: 5dfa8f5 content on main, with revert
              commit 7a11af8 on top. Tests + lint + build all pass.
              Option C functional — every override-flag mutation now
              writes a PATROL_SCHEDULE:OVERRIDE_CONFLICT audit row
              with the correct tenant guard intact.

              KNOWN RESIDUAL CODE SMELL (acceptable):
                `writeAuditLog(prisma as unknown as any, {...})` —
                two occurrences in patrolSchedule.ts override paths
                (L138, L221 region). Not a security issue. Could be
                tightened to `as unknown as PrismaClient` if revisited.

              KNOWN RESIDUAL BEHAVIOR (acceptable):
                Non-atomic create→audit on override path. If the audit
                INSERT fails after the schedule INSERT succeeds, the
                override happens without audit trail. Same DB
                connection / immediate sequential write makes this
                vanishingly unlikely in practice. A future polish dispatch
                could wrap in `prisma.$transaction` (the tenant-guarded
                instance — NOT platformPrisma) if the cast can be
                resolved cleanly; needs investigation of why Sonnet
                couldn't make the tx type match writeAuditLog's signature
                without escaping to platformPrisma.
